title: Harbor Crest Realty website visual refresh: license one large Adobe Stock architecture hero, brand-grade it, crop/reframe into desktop + tablet + mobile heroes, section headers and banner ads, and vectorize the logo for placement
one_line_ask: License one large Adobe Stock architecture photograph, brand-grade it to the Harbor Crest palette, then crop/reframe that single graded master into a desktop hero, tablet hero, mobile hero, two section-header strips, and two promo banner sizes, vectorize the client logo, and package the cohesive web-ready set with a font recommendation and a review board.

issues: Slight visible moiré pattern/screen lines from photographing a monitor (expected for a client-supplied raster asset, not penalized per brief).
Background is a light gray but not perfectly uniform due to screen/photo artifacts (acceptable for a source asset).
Minor shadow/edge at the bottom from the laptop bezel (not affecting main asset).
Slight luminance/scan-line texture visible on screen surface (acceptable client-supplied artifact)
Image is a photo of a screen rather than a direct export, introducing minor moire/pixel row banding (within acceptable source-file tolerance)
Slight warm color cast overall (golden hour intentional but slightly heavy on amber tones in floor/ceiling — designer should color-balance for neutral deliverables)
Stone feature wall texture reads slightly flat/even — minor CGI suspicion but within acceptable photorealism range
Artwork on wall is a blank canvas/placeholder — acceptable as source asset, designer must replace or clear
Near-white sofa and rug tones are very close in luminance — print-unsafe without separation adjustment
Pendant lamp partially cropped at top-left edge — minor compositional note for tight crops
Glass balustrade reflection is clean but slightly too perfect — borderline CGI indicator, not penalizable
